rulings.Case Title: JOHN A KITHAS VS. LARRY WONG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order For Sale Of Dwelling
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 28, 2022 line 1 PETITIONER JOHN KITHAS Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order For Sale Of Dwelling Hearing Required. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: WALTER A. MACDONALD, JR. ET AL VS. ROXANNE C. LOCHER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlemnet Agreement
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 28, 2022 line 3 DEFENDANT ROXANNE C. LOCHER, SUCCESSOR TRUSTEE UNDER THE Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlemnet Agreement Hearing Required. By no later than 4:30 p.m. on July 27, 2022 Macdonald to call California Bar Ethics Hotline and obtain advice on his law firm serving as escrow company in this case. By no later than 9:00 a.m. on July 28, 2022 MacDonald to file a declaration stating (1) that he asked for advice, (2) that he provided all relevant information, (including, but no limited to, the facts that MacDonald is a party to the action and to the settlement agreement; that he is required to perform under the agreement and that he chose his law firm as the escrow company) and (3) whether selection of his law firm was approved by the State Bar Ethics Hotline or not (details are not necessary to be included in the declaration) or, if response is not immediately available, the date such response will be provided to MacDonald by the Hotline. Furthermore, MacDonald is to obtain at least one quote for title insurance from a company ready and willing to provide it. Declaration shall include information regarding quote and the name of the company. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SHERRY JACKSON VS. TIMOTHY JENKINS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 28, 2022 line 4. DEFENDANT TIMOTHY JENKINS, CARMEN JENKINS, MONICA JENKINS Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint is DENIED without prejudice. Motion contains no evidence upon which the Court may base a determination that the delay in filing a cross-complaint was in good faith. No facts supporting oversight, inadvertence, mistake, neglect provided. Conclusory, self-serving declaration of counsel without facts is insufficient. The Court notes that no proposed cross-complaint was attached to the motion and no reply was filed. The Court notes that the moving party failed to comply with SFLR 2.B. Failure to comply in the future will result in motions being taken off calendar. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ANTONIO C. GILTON ET AL VS. VELOCITY COMMERCIAL CAPITAL, LLC, A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 28, 2022 line 5. DEFENDANT SPFF LP, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED PARTNERSHIP DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT is sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to bring this action in the name of Antonio Gilton, as trustee. (Opposition, but not the operative complaint suggest that Antonio is the current successor trustee; Antonio is currently a plaintiff in his individual capacity only). The Court notes that while Plaintiffs assert in opposition standing to sue in their individual capacities and beneficiaries, this assertion is not supported by any citation to authority. Demurrer is further sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege a wrongdoing by the moving defendant giving rise to any of the asserted causes of action. Plaintiff shall further allege facts showing that the moving defendant is not a bona fide purchaser. And if such facts may not be alleged in good faith, allege facts showing that the sale is void as to a bona fide purchaser. Demurrer based on res judicata is overruled. The complaint contains allegations of impropriety as to the loan origination. Such allegations could not and were not a part of the determination in any unlawful detainer judgment. The Court further notes that Doris Gilton, was sued in her individual capacity in the UD action and the judgment was entered against her, as an individual. Defendant failed to establish privity at this stage of the litigation (legal authority and/or facts on the face of the complaint) between the owner Trust and Doris Gilton in her individual capacity sufficient for res judicata. The Court notes that opposing party failed to comply with SFLR 2.B. Failure to comply in the future will result in motions being treated as unopposed. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
